content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion as a core vocabulary for high school English writing workshops trains students to revise drafts by reflecting on inconsistent logic and poorly reasoned arguments, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow the structured, educator-designed reflective writing exercises from high school literacy curricula to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Specifically, the scaffolded reflection prompts used to teach student writers to self-assess can be adapted to create multi-step, perspective-aligned self-reflection routines for LLMs that outperform unstructured self-reflection approaches.
